Chumor 1.0: Ruiqi He ... Naihao Deng; 1,951 RZB jokes with human-annotated explanations; native-speaker A/B testing; 'LLMs win in 2-3% of cases' (GPT-4o 2.2%, ERNIE 2.9%) confirmed verbatim. Guide's direct+CoT / 10 LLMs / above-random claims correctly belong to 2.0.

notes: Chumor 2.0: ten LLMs, direct + CoT prompting; best accuracy 60.3% ('10 points above random', human 78.3%; best MCC 0.29 vs human 0.60); A/B LLM-wins 2-3% confirmed verbatim.

discrepancies.guide_says: multi-task across crosstalk generation, recognition, type/level classification, and punchline detection
discrepancies.paper_says: CFunModel's tasks are Crosstalk Response Selection, Humor Recognition, Joke Generation/Continuation, Humor Explanation, and Crosstalk Generation (Table 1; Sec 4). 'Humor type classification, humor level classification, and punchline detection' appear only in Related Work as tasks proposed by Chen et al. (2024a) — not tasks of this paper's model.
CFunSet comprises 'more than 160,000 entries'; the 20,000 figure is only the Tieba-JokeBar subset ('over 20,000 jokes collected from Tieba—JokeBar').

notes: Findings claim ('surpasses general LLMs on Chinese humor recognition') and year/preprint status confirmed; only the method characterization has issues.
Ma, Huang, Xu, Zhou, Wei; ACL 2025 SRW; Constant-Variable Optimization; Overlap metric; near-impossible framing; 'strategy helps stronger models most' confirmed.
Su, Zhu, Chen, Benavides Prado, Witbrock; HDM (CoT + humor theory); gains 7.75%/2.81%/6.13% humour/fluency/coherence (guide ~7.8%/~2.8%/~6.1%) confirmed verbatim.

Pituxcoosuvarn & Murakami; Digital (MDPI) 2025 5(4):49; NMT vs GPT/GPT-P/GPT-Ex EN->Thai; McNemar; GPT-Ex 62.94% vs MT 50.12% confirmed. Nuance (not flagged): baseline GPT (41.06%) underperforms MT; the guide correctly centers GPT-Ex, matching the paper's own abstract framing.
